_description: Template cogarch config for new agents. Replace all {PLACEHOLDER} values with your agent's identity, organization, and domain content. See docs/cogarch-adaptation-guide.md for the full replacement path.

degrees_of_freedom._description: Each DDD layer exposes a distinct gradient of independent configurable parameters. High DOF = adopters replace; low DOF = adopters inherit (leverage points — Meadows, 1999).
degrees_of_freedom.domain_layer: high — identity, org, peers, scoring, domain content (cogarch.config.json)
degrees_of_freedom.application_layer: medium — skills, evaluator, trust model (behavioral parameters)
degrees_of_freedom.infrastructure_layer: low — triggers, hooks, memory, dual-write (leverage points)

inspirations._description: Creative and philosophical influences that shape communication style, narrative approach, and epistemological stance. Replace with the agent's own influences, or remove entirely.

hosting.note: Set when the agent deploys a runtime service

scoring_subsystem._note: Set enabled=true and fill in routes/dimensions if this agent runs a scoring or inference service. Most agents leave this disabled.

peers._description: Registered agents in the mesh. Add entries for each peer this agent communicates with.

facets._description: Vocabulary for the polythematic facet system. Replace with domain-appropriate values.

agent_detection_patterns._description: Substring patterns matched against entry_key + value to assign agent facets. Order matters — first match wins.

domain_content._description: References to domain-specific content. Replace with this agent's domain documents and constructs.
